title: astragalus asterias subsp. astraboides
intro: Astragalus asterias subsp. astraboides, also known as Astragalus, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to the Mediterranean region and is used as a substitute for the more commonly used Astragalus membranaceus. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its traditional use in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Astragalus include boosting the immune system, reducing stress and fatigue, and improving cardiovascular health. Astragalus can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
